title: Open-Hardware Mobile Collection
description: Keep in Survey: the open-hardware question is a data-value-per-setup-hour decision, not a build commitment.
summary: Survey-stage consolidation of Notion open-hardware and mobile data-collection ideas: decide which low-cost robot arms, hands, mobile bases, and sensing rigs can become a realistic collection platform rather than a hardware distraction.

details.text: Bimanual open-hardware idea: compare OpenArm, AmazingHand, Calibur-like controllers, and other low-cost platforms as data-collection devices for VLA/VLN. The dashboard already tracks real-robot infra, so this survey should only keep candidates that change data scale or embodiment coverage.

details: Mobile platform idea: triangular omniwheel balancing base, dual-arm frame, and lift stage for collection in human environments. This needs a BOM/safety/teleop feasibility pass before it becomes engineering.

Dexterous-hand hardware ideas folded here: water-cooled dense-motor hand, fingernail-in-fingertip for picking, dexterous hand hanging objects on rack, humanoid acrobatics, and multi-robot contact cooperation. These should be triaged by cost, controllability, safety, and whether they unlock a specific dataset.

details.text: Adjacent reference space includes low-cost mobile/bimanual robots and open-source humanoids; use them as feasibility comparators, not as automatic build targets.

Notion migration addendum 2026-05-31: open-hardware dual-arm with OpenArm + AmazingHand + Calibur-like controllers, mobile collection base, dense-motor/water-cooled hand, fingernail fingertip picking, dexterous rack-hanging tasks, humanoid acrobatics, and multi-robot contact cooperation are retained only as build/no-build candidates.

risks_decisions: Hardware ideas are easy to over-expand. The survey must score candidates by data value per setup hour, not just novelty.
Anything involving sharp tools, high force, mobile bases, or acrobatics stays out of active engineering until safety and supervision constraints are explicit.
If a platform cannot produce calibrated observations/actions compatible with UMI/HOI/VTLA state schemas, it should remain archived/reference-only.
First deliverable is a decision table and one recommended build/no-build call.

Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because existing open-hand/mobile robot papers provide candidates but do not decide which platform, if any, improves our data collection enough to justify setup cost.
Next executable analysis: score each candidate by cost, build risk, sensing, calibration, safety, UMI/HOI/VTLA compatibility, and expected unique data.
Move standalone LEAP-hand glove ideas to Archive as external/covered hardware references, while keeping this card for platform-level build/no-build decisions.
The migration audit keeps shaol/3D-printing paused separately; this card is only for data-collection hardware ROI, not for committing to every interesting robot build.
